description: county seat of Union County, South Carolina
description_wiki: The city of Union is the county seat of Union County, South Carolina, United States.

description: city in Rowan County, North Carolina, United States
description_wiki: Salisbury ( SAWLZ-ber-ee) is a city in the Piedmont region of North Carolina, United States; it has been the county seat of Rowan County since 1753 when its territory extended to the Mississippi River.

description: railway company in the United States, active 1894–1990
description_wiki: The Southern Railway (also known as Southern Railway Company; reporting mark SOU) was a class 1 railroad based in the Southern United States between 1894 and 1982, at which time it merged with the Norfolk and Western Railway (N&W) to form the Norfolk Southern Railway.

description: historically black institution in Salisbury, North Carolina
description_wiki: Livingstone College is a private historically black Christian college in Salisbury, North Carolina.

description: maritime branch of the United States military
description_wiki: The United States Navy (USN) is the maritime service branch of the United States Armed Forces and is designated as the navy of the United States in the Constitution.

description: seat of San Diego County, California, United States; second-largest city in California
description_wiki: San Diego (  SAN dee-AY-goh; Spanish: [san ˈdjeɣo]) is a city on the Pacific coast of Southern California, adjacent to the Mexico–United States border.

description: 1945 Albany-class cruiser
description_wiki: USS Albany (CA-123) was a United States Navy Oregon City-class heavy cruiser, later converted to the guided missile cruiser CG-10.

aliases: War to Resist US Aggression and Aid Korea
description: war between North and South Korea, 1950–1953
description_wiki: The Korean War (25 June 1950 – 27 July 1953) was an armed conflict the Korean Peninsula fought between North Korea (Democratic People's Republic of Korea; DPRK) and South Korea (Republic of Korea; ROK) and their allies.

description: county seat of Escambia County, Florida, United States
description_wiki: Pensacola ( PEN-sə-KOH-lə) is a city in the Florida panhandle in the United States.

surface_forms: naval air station base there, Mainside
description: airport
description_wiki: Naval Air Station Pensacola or NAS Pensacola (IATA: NPA, ICAO: KNPA, FAA LID: NPA) is a United States Navy base located next to Warrington, Florida, a community southwest of the Pensacola city limits.

description: city in Winston County, Mississippi, United States of America
description_wiki: Louisville (pronounced LEW-iss-vill) is a city in and the county seat of Winston County, Mississippi.

surface_forms: Norfolk Naval Air Station in Norfolk, Virginia
description: United States Navy shipyard in Virginia
description_wiki: The Norfolk Naval Shipyard, often called the Norfolk Navy Yard and abbreviated as NNSY, is a U.S. Navy facility in Portsmouth, Virginia.

description: independent city in Virginia, United States
description_wiki: Norfolk (locally   NOR-fək) is an independent city in the U.S. commonwealth of Virginia.

description: United States hospital administered by the VA
county seat of Mahoning County, Ohio, United States
description_wiki: Youngstown is a city in Mahoning County, Ohio, United States, and the county seat (however, a small portion of the city is in Trumbull County).

description: city in and county seat of King County, State of Washington, United States
description_wiki: Seattle (  see-AT-əl) is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Washington and the Pacific Northwest region of North America.

entity: United States Public Health Service Hospital
surface_forms: US Public Health Service Hospital here
description: division of the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services concerned with public health
description_wiki: The United States Public Health Service (USPHS or PHS) is a collection of agencies of the United States Department of Health and Human Services which manages public health, containing nine out of the department's twelve operating divisions.

description: American music supervisor, music director, conductor, vocal and dance arranger, pianist and actor
description_wiki: David Loud (born November 28, 1961, in Cincinnati, Ohio) is an American music supervisor, music director, conductor, vocal and dance arranger, pianist and actor.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Texas (  TEK-səss) is the most populous state in the Southern United States.

description: Canadian politician (1936-2012)
description_wiki: Robert Philip "Bob" Kaplan,  (December 27, 1936 – November 5, 2012) was a Canadian politician and lawyer.

description: 40th presidential administration and cabinet of the USA (1981-1989)
description_wiki: Ronald Reagan's tenure as the 40th president of the United States began with his first inauguration on January 20, 1981, and ended on January 20, 1989.

description: American politician (1905–1989)
description_wiki: Warren Grant Magnuson (April 12, 1905 – May 20, 1989) was an American lawyer and politician who represented the state of Washington in Congress for 44 years, first as a representative from 1937 to 1944, and then as a senator from 1944 to 1981.

description: mayor of Seattle, Washington
description_wiki: Charles Theodore Royer (August 22, 1939 – July 26, 2024) was an American news reporter and politician who served as the 48th mayor of Seattle, Washington from 1978 to 1990.
